spec.reasoning: I chose lexical renames and a distractor column placed after the last column. The distractor column is inserted AFTER the last column (column 76+) so it does NOT shift any referenced columns (AD-AO, W, L, J, M-P). This avoids the failure from Attempt 1 where the distractor was inserted within the referenced range. The lexical renames change 'Old Rates' to 'Previous Rates', 'New Rates' to 'Updated Rates', and 'Annually' to 'Yearly' — all semantically equivalent at the same descriptiveness level. Since 'Annually' appears as data values in column I, I include data_value_renames. The sheet is renamed from 'Data' to 'Pricing'. None of these renames affect the instruction text since the instruction references columns by letter/position rather than by header name. The distractor column 'Rate Adjustment Notes' is plausibly related to the pricing domain but clearly different from existing columns.
